What's wrong?

How could you possibly get married back then if you didn't even get along?
There must surely be a reason for this.

I said, I'll tell you something—what's the point? Their ages didn't match; they were over ten years apart. They got married, but less than a month later, they divorced.
Who knows? You two get along well, so I'm asking you about everything.
It's not easy to arrange a meeting alone, is it?
It's not Xiao Hong. Didn't I say you should live closer to the subway? Your place is three kilometers from the station. It's inconvenient for me to commute to work.
You're facing east, but you're looking at others' windows, which are so big. Yours is just this small. Since you're facing east, I'll just place an eye to watch the sunrise.

It's not a mandatory requirement, is it? I just want to live in a better place. Look at this wall—everything's falling apart. And then there's the smell of the five flavors. Can't you try to find me another house?
You're wrong to talk like that. I'm the one paying you to help me. Don't go looking for another car.

It's not that I'm certain I'll definitely be able to afford a good place—just go and look around, there are so many options nearby. Why do you insist on this particular neighborhood? Is that really all the capability you have—just finding me this kind of house?
Aren't you the one who can't smell it? That pungent odor is so strong I'd get sick just walking in here—whose responsibility would it be if I fell ill?
Alternatively, let's sign a contract: if you spend money to send me to treat my illness, I'll repay you for the rest of my life.
Enough, enough. I've seen your abilities—pretty much that's all you've got. Forget it, really.
Ah, look at this room—it's just the one that was recently vacated on this side.

It's not that it's mainly because housing is currently so tight; this room is actually quite good. Look at the environment—it's excellent. The windows face south and are all according to your requirements, aren't they?

I'm telling you seriously—if I had to introduce these clients of yours every month, I'd be fed up myself, you know?

But look, I'll find you a subway-proximate one. You say the cost is too high and it's not suitable. If I find one farther away, you complain that the walls are newly painted. How about a motorhome? How should it be arranged?
